summary: A presidential official proposed redistributing AI-driven tax revenue to tackle wealth inequality.
The policy idea draws criticism as a populist gamble with uncertain economic consequences.
Debate centers on whether AI gains should fund social programs or fuel investment.
editorial.stance: Editorial questions the feasibility and wisdom of redistributing AI-generated revenues without deeper structural reform.
summaryTr: 대통령 관계자가 부의 불평등에 대처하기 위해 AI 기반 세수를 재분배할 것을 제안했다.
이 정책 아이디어는 불확실한 경제적 결과를 담은 포퓰리즘적 도박이라는 비판을 받고 있다.
AI로 인한 이득이 사회 프로그램에 자금을 공급해야 하는지, 아니면 투자를 촉진해야 하는지를 놓고 논쟁이 벌어지고 있다.

editorial.title: 일본인 여행객 친 음주운전자 5년...선진국형 양형 검토를
editorial.body: (사설) 서울 음주운전 사망사고로 일본인 관광객 모녀 중 어머니가 숨진 사건에서 30대 남성이 징역 5년을 선고받았다. 음주운전 처벌 수위, 교통사고 특례법, 국민 법 감정, 선진국형 양형 기준이 논의되고 있다. |
summary: 서울 음주운전 사망사고로 일본인 관광객 모녀 중 어머니가 숨졌다.
가해자 30대 남성이 징역 5년을 선고받았다.
음주운전 처벌 수위와 선진국형 양형 기준 논의가 필요하다.
editorial.pullQuote: 음주운전 처벌 수위, 교통사고 특례법, 국민 법 감정, 선진국형 양형 기준이 논의되고 있다.

A man in his 30s, the perpetrator, was sentenced to five years in prison.
There is a need for discussion on the severity of drunk driving penalties and sentencing standards based on developed countries' models.

editorial.body: 삼성 반도체 파업 강행 땐 긴급조정권 발동할 수밖에 정부가 중재한 삼성전자 노사의 사후 조정이 끝내 결렬됐다. 17시간에 걸친 마라톤 협상을 벌였지만 성과급 산정 방식 등을 둘러싼 이견을 좁히지 못했다. 이제 삼성전자 노조가 예고한 18일간 총파업이 눈앞으로 다
editorial.pullQuote: 17시간에 걸친 마라톤 협상을 벌였지만 성과급 산정 방식 등을 둘러싼 이견을 좁히지 못했다.
summary: 삼성전자 노사 정부 중재 협상이 결렬됐다.
성과급 산정 방식 등 이견을 좁히지 못했다.

Disputes over performance bonus calculation methods and other issues remain unresolved.

editorials.title: [사설] 與 후보 “대장동은 창의적 모델” 전국에 ‘대장동’ 공약하길
editorials.body: 與 후보 대장동은 창의적 모델 전국에 대장동 공약하길 민주당 박찬대 인천시장 후보가 대장동 모델을 언급하면서 그런 창의적인 아이디어가 많이 요구된다고 말했다. 박 후보는 기업에 정당한 이익을 보장하되, 초과 이익은 공공 몫으로 환수하는 설계가 핵심이라고 했다..
editorials.pullQuote: 기업에 정당한 이익을 보장하되, 초과 이익은 공공 몫으로 환수하는 설계가 핵심
summary: 민주당 박찬대 인천시장 후보가 대장동을 창의적 모델로 평가했다.
기업 이익 보장하되 초과 이익은 공공이 환수하는 구조가 핵심이다.
전국 확대를 위한 공약으로 제시되고 있다.
editorials.stance: 대장동 모델의 창의성을 인정하며 전국 확대 가능성을 제기
editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Ruling Party Candidate Calls Daejangdong a "Creative Model," Pushes It as National Policy
summaryTr: Democratic Party's Incheon mayoral candidate Park Chan-dae evaluated Daejangdong as a creative model.
The core structure guarantees corporate profits while allowing the public to recoup excess gains.
It is being presented as a campaign pledge for nationwide expansion.

editorials.title: [사설] 세금으로 북 축구팀 응원 지원, 사용처 왜 안 밝히나
editorials.body: 세금으로 북 축구팀 응원 지원, 사용처 왜 안 밝히나 정부가 아시아축구연맹 경기 참가를 위해 17일 방남하는 북한 여자 축구팀을 응원하는 대북 민간 단체에 남북협력기금 3억원을 지급한다고 밝혔다. 통일부는 이번 행사가 남북 상호 이해 증진에 기여한다는 점을 고려
editorials.pullQuote: 정부가 아시아축구연맹 경기 참가를 위해 방남하는 북한 여자 축구팀을 응원하는 데 남북협력기금 3억원을 지급한다.
summary: 정부가 북한 여자 축구팀 응원에 남북협력기금 3억원을 지급한다.
통일부는 남북 상호 이해 증진에 기여한다고 설명했다.
그러나 세금 사용처가 투명하게 공개되지 않아 논란이다.
editorials.stance: 공적 자금의 사용처 공개와 투명성이 필수적이어야 한다.
editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Why Hide Where Tax Money for North Korea Soccer Support Goes?
summaryTr: The government will provide 300 million won from the inter-Korean cooperation fund to support North Korea's women's soccer team.
The Unification Ministry explained that it contributes to promoting mutual understanding between the two Koreas.
However, controversy has arisen as the use of tax money has not been disclosed transparently.
editorials.bodyTr: The government announced it will provide 300 million won from the inter-Korean cooperation fund to civic groups supporting North Korea's women's soccer team, which will visit the South on the 17th to participate in Asian Football Confederation matches. The Unification Ministry cited considerations that the event will contribute to promoting mutual understanding between the two Koreas.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative

editorial.title: [사설] 정부, 긴급조정권 써서라도 삼성전자 파업 막아야
editorial.body: 입력 종합 26면 파업을 막기 위한 정부의 중재 시도가 무산됐다. 최승호 초기업노조 삼성전자지부 위원장이 17시간의 협상을 마치고 13일 새벽 세종시 정부세종청사 중앙노동위원회에서 2차 사후조정 결렬을 선언했다. 연합뉴스 삼성전자의 파업을 막기 위한 정부의 중재 시도가 무산됐다. 이틀에 걸친 삼성전자 노사 협상이 어제 새벽 최종 결렬됐다. 오는 21일로 예고된 파업이 초읽기에 들어갔다. 정부는 어제 김민석 총리 주재로 긴급 관계장관회의를 열고 대응 방향을 논의했다. 김 총리는 “어떠한 경우에도 파업으로 이어지지 않게끔 노사 간의 대화가 지속적으로 이루어지도록 적극 지원해야 한다”고 관계 부처에 당부했다. 정부의 최종 조정안이 제시되기도 전에 노동조합이 결렬을 선언하고 협상장을 떠나는 바람에 정부의 사후조정 절차가 중단된 건 매우 유감스럽다. 노사는 막판까지 파국을 막기 위한 물밑 교섭을 회피하지 말기 바란다. 이제는 정부도 긴급조정권 발동을 포함해 이번 파업 사태에 직접 개입하는 방안을 검토할 때가 됐다. 긴급조정권은 노동조합 및 노동관계조정법 76조에 근거해 쟁의행위가 국민의 일상을 위태롭게 하거나 국민 경제를 현저히 해할 우려가 있을 때 노동부 장관이 발동할 수 있는 예외적 조정 절차다. 긴급조정권이 발동되면 노조는 즉시 쟁의행위를 중지해야 하고, 30일간 쟁의행위를 재개할 수 없으며, 중앙노동위원회의 직권조정을 수용해야 한다. 자율적이어야 하는 노사 협상에 정부가 개입하는 것은 가능하면 피하는 것이 옳다. 그동안 정부가 긴급조정권 발동을 자제해 온 것도 이런 이유 때문일 것이다. 지금까지 긴급조정권이 발동된 사례는 1969년 대한조선공사 파업과 1993년 현대자동차 파업, 2005년 7월과 12월 아시아나항공·대한항공 조종사 파업 네 차례뿐이다. 김영훈 노동부 장관도 “대화로 해결해야 한다”며 긴급조정권 발동에 신중한 입장을 밝혔다. 하지만 모처럼 찾아온 반도체 초호황기에 고객사가 이탈하고 공급망이 훼손되는 최악의 사태를 방치하는 것은 국가의 책임 방기가 아닐 수 없다. 삼성전자 파업으로 벌어질 반도체 생산 중단 등에 따른 직접적인 피해만 40조원으로 추산된다. 반도체는 우리 수출의 3분의 1을 차지하는 국가 기간산업이다. 정부가 긴급조정권을 검토한다는 시그널만으로도 노사가 막판 협상에 나서도록 압박하는 수단이 될 수 있다. 삼성전자 파업이 초래할 파국은 반드시 피해야 한다.
editorial.pullQuote: 모처럼 찾아온 반도체 초호황기에 고객사가 이탈하고 공급망이 훼손되는 최악의 사태를 방치하는 것은 국가의 책임 방기가 아닐 수 없다.
summary: 삼성전자 노사협상이 결렬되고 21일 파업이 임박했다.
정부는 긴급조정권 발동을 포함한 직접 개입을 검토해야 한다.
반도체 산업 피해 40조원, 국가 책임 회피할 수 없다.
editorial.stance: 정부는 국가 기간산업 보호를 위해 긴급조정권 발동도 불사해야 한다.
editorial.titleTr: Editorial: Government Must Use Emergency Adjustment Powers to Stop Samsung Electronics Strike
summaryTr: Samsung Electronics labor negotiations have collapsed, with a strike imminent on the 21st.
The government must consider direct intervention, including invoking emergency adjustment powers.
With 40 trillion won in damages to the semiconductor industry, the state cannot shirk its responsibility.
editorial.bodyTr:
Government mediation efforts to prevent a strike at Samsung Electronics have fallen through. After 17 hours of negotiations, Choi Seung-ho, chairman of the Korean Metal Workers' Union's Samsung Electronics division, declared a breakdown in secondary mediation at the Central Labor Relations Commission in the Government Sejong Complex early on the 13th. A two-day round of labor-management negotiations at Samsung Electronics concluded in breakdown early yesterday. A strike scheduled for the 21st is now imminent. The government held an emergency cabinet meeting presided over by Prime Minister Kim Min-seok yesterday to discuss response measures. The prime minister instructed relevant ministries that "in any circumstance, we must actively support ongoing dialogue between labor and management to prevent the situation from escalating into a strike."

It is deeply regrettable that the union declared a breakdown and left the negotiating table before the government could even present its final mediation proposal, thereby halting the government's post-adjustment procedures. Labor and management should refrain from avoiding behind-the-scenes negotiations to prevent a catastrophic outcome until the very end. Now is the time for the government to also consider directly intervening in this strike situation, including the exercise of emergency adjustment authority. Emergency adjustment authority is an exceptional mediation procedure that the labor minister can invoke under Article 76 of the Trade Union and Labor Relations Adjustment Act when collective action threatens the daily lives of citizens or risks causing significant harm to the national economy. When emergency adjustment authority is invoked, unions must immediately cease collective action, cannot resume such action for 30 days, and must accept the direct adjustment of the Central Labor Relations Commission.

It is appropriate to avoid government intervention in labor-management negotiations, which should be autonomous by nature. The government's restraint in exercising emergency adjustment authority has likely stemmed from this principle. To date, emergency adjustment authority has been invoked only four times: the 1969 Korea Shipbuilding and Offshore Engineering strike, the 1993 Hyundai Motor strike, and the Asiana Airlines and Korean Air pilot strikes in July and December 2005. Labor Minister Kim Young-hoon also indicated a cautious stance toward invoking emergency adjustment authority, saying that "the matter should be resolved through dialogue."

However, allowing the worst-case scenario of customer defections and supply chain damage during this rare semiconductor boom would constitute an abdication of state responsibility. Direct damages from Samsung Electronics strike-induced semiconductor production halts alone are estimated at 40 trillion won. Semiconductors account for one-third of our exports and are a vital national industry. The government's mere signal that it is considering emergency adjustment authority could serve as pressure to push labor and management back to last-minute negotiations. The catastrophe that a Samsung Electronics strike would bring must be prevented at all costs.

editorial.title: 'Citizen dividend'
editorial.body: Top presidential aide's proposal raises fears of anti-market thinking Kim Yong-beon, the presidential chief of staff for policy, on Tuesday proposed a "citizen dividend" scheme that would return part of the windfall tax revenue generated by the semiconductor boom to the public. After the proposal triggered controversy, the presidential office brushed it off as Kim’s personal opinion that had not been discussed internally. Nevertheless, the abrupt and radical suggestion from the government’s top policymaker bewildered people who place their trust in a capitalist market economy. Kim wrote on Facebook that the fruits of the artificial intelligence infrastructure era are not generated by a handful of companies alone, but rest on an industrial foundation built collectively by the Korean people over the past half-century, adding that part of those gains should be systematically returned to the public. He mentioned "citizen dividends" as a concrete way of putting the idea into practice. First, the anti-market undertones contained in the concept of distributing profits in the form of dividends to every citizen are deeply concerning. The argument that semiconductor companies have grown on the back of special legislation, public infrastructure such as roads, electricity and water supply, and tax benefits is understandable to some extent. But that is entirely different from saying that those profits should therefore be distributed to the public. In 2023, Samsung Electronics suffered a 15 trillion won ($10 billion) loss in its semiconductor business alone. The government and the public did not absorb any of those losses then. A structure in which corporate "windfall profits" are shared with the public, while companies alone bear the losses, is inconsistent with both fairness and basic economic principles. Instead, Kim’s proposal gives the impression that the government also intends to take a slice of the hundreds of trillions of won in operating profits expected this year at Samsung Electronics and SK hynix, as labor unions, subcontractors, farmers and fisher groups, and even politicians, scramble for a share. When companies earn profits, the government collects its due in corporate taxes. This is the mechanism through which profits are redistributed to society in a capitalist market economy. If the government seeks to take corporate profits by means other than taxes, it would need a legal basis enacted through legislation. Otherwise, it would amount to unfair double taxation, nearing coercion. If there is excess tax revenue, it should primarily be used to pay down the national debt in line with the principles of fiscal management. Redistributing excess tax revenue to the people under the label of a "dividend" invites suspicions of a populist attempt at winning favor with cash payments. Companies are fulfilling their social obligations by paying taxes and creating jobs. If a company still has profits left after covering its costs, including taxes, debt repayments, payments to subcontractors and labor expenses, those profits belong to the shareholders. If profits are to be "shared with society," the decision likewise belongs to the shareholders. Viewed from the perspective of enhancing industrial competitiveness, Kim’s understanding of the issue is overly complacent. Not only is the AI-driven semiconductor boom unlikely to continue forever, but major competitors such as the United States and China are aggressively closing the gap. In these circumstances, is it not the role of the president’s chief economic aide to worry first about strategies for maintaining or expanding the lead? For tech companies, profits should be used first and foremost for investment in new technologies and facilities. Companies that fail to do so are quickly driven out of the market. Now is not the time to kill the goose that lays the golden eggs, but to raise more such geese. Foreign investors reportedly dumped Korean stocks in large quantities on Tuesday after Kim’s remarks. His words may not have been the sole reason for the sell-off, but markets are highly sensitive to such signals. One of the government’s responsibilities is to safeguard the principles of the market economy so people can invest with confidence. editorial.pullQuote: A structure where corporate 'windfall profits' are shared with the public, while companies alone bear losses, is inconsistent with fairness.
summary: Presidential aide Kim Yong-beon proposed a 'citizen dividend' scheme to share semiconductor windfall profits with the public.
Editorial warns the plan threatens market principles and unfairly shifts corporate profits away from shareholders.
Critics argue profits should fund R&D and debt, not populist cash payments, as global competition intensifies.
editorial.stance: The editorial opposes the citizen dividend proposal as anti-market, economically unsound, and strategically misguided during global tech competition.
summaryTr: Presidential aide Kim Yong-beon proposed a 'citizen dividend' scheme to share semiconductor windfall profits with the public.
Editorial warns the plan threatens market principles and unfairly shifts corporate profits away from shareholders.
Critics argue profits should fund R&D and debt, not populist cash payments, as global competition intensifies.
editorial.bodyTr:
대통령 정책실장의 제안이 반시장적 사고에 대한 우려를 야기하다

정책실장 김용빈이 지난 화요일 반도체 호황으로 인한 임시세수의 일부를 국민에게 돌려주는 '국민배당금' 제도를 제안했다. 논란이 일자 대통령실은 이를 내부 논의 없는 김 실장의 개인 의견이라며 제쳐 두었다. 그럼에도 불구하고 정부의 최고 정책 담당자로부터 나온 이 급작스럽고 급진적인 제안은 자본주의 시장경제를 신뢰하는 국민들을 혼란스럽게 했다.

김 실장은 페이스북에 인공지능 인프라 시대의 과실이 소수 기업만으로 만들어진 것이 아니라 지난 반세기 동안 국민이 집단적으로 구축한 산업 기반 위에 이루어진다며, 이러한 이득의 일부가 국민에게 체계적으로 돌아가야 한다고 썼다. 그는 이러한 생각을 구체화하는 방법으로 '국민배당금'을 제시했다.

먼저 모든 국민에게 배당금 형태로 이윤을 배분한다는 개념에 담긴 반시장적 톤은 깊은 우려를 낳는다. 반도체 기업들이 특별 입법, 도로·전기·수도 등 공공 인프라, 그리고 조세 혜택을 바탕으로 성장했다는 주장은 어느 정도 이해할 수 있다. 하지만 그렇기 때문에 그 이윤을 국민에게 배분해야 한다는 것은 전혀 다른 문제다. 2023년 삼성전자는 반도체 사업만 해도 15조 원의 손실을 입었다. 당시 정부와 국민은 이 손실의 어떤 부분도 흡수하지 않았다. 기업의 '초과 이윤'은 국민과 공유하되 손실은 기업이 단독으로 감당하는 구조는 공정성과 기본적인 경제 원칙 모두에 부합하지 않는다.

오히려 김 실장의 제안은 정부 역시 올해 삼성전자와 SK하이닉스에서 예상되는 수백조 원대의 영업이익에서 한몫을 챙히려 한다는 인상을 준다. 노조, 협력업체, 농어민, 심지어 정치인들까지 한몫을 차지하려고 몸부림치는 가운데 말이다.

기업이 이윤을 얻으면 정부는 법인세 형태로 정당한 몫을 걷는다. 이것이 자본주의 시장경제에서 이윤이 사회에 재분배되는 메커니즘이다. 만약 정부가 세금 이외의 방법으로 기업 이윤을 취하려 한다면 입법을 통해 제정된 법적 근거가 필요하다. 그렇지 않으면 이중과세에 해당하며 강압에 가까워진다.

만약 초과세수가 존재한다면 먼저 재정 원칙에 따라 국가채무 상환에 사용되어야 한다. 초과세수를 '배당금'이라는 이름으로 국민에게 재분배하는 것은 현금 살포로 호감을 사려는 포퓰리즘적 시도라는 의혹을 낳는다.

기업은 세금을 내고 일자리를 창출함으로써 사회적 의무를 다하고 있다. 비용(세금, 채무 상환, 협력업체 지급금, 노동비용 포함)을 충당한 후 남은 이윤은 주주의 것이다. 만약 이윤을 '사회와 공유'하려 한다면, 그 결정 역시 주주에게 속한다.

산업 경쟁력 강화 관점에서 보면 김 실장의 이해는 지나치게 안일하다. AI 주도 반도체 호황이 영원히 계속될 리 없을 뿐 아니라 미국과 중국 같은 주요 경쟁국들이 격차를 빠르게 따라잡고 있다. 이러한 상황에서 대통령의 최고 경제 보좌관의 역할이 우위를 유지하거나 확대하기 위한 전략을 먼저 고민하는 것 아닌가?

기술기업에게는 이윤을 우선 새로운 기술과 시설 투자에 사용해야 한다. 그렇게 하지 못하는 기업은 금방 시장에서 도태된다. 지금은 황금알을 낳는 거위를 죽일 때가 아니라 더 많은 거위를 길러낼 때다.

외국인 투자자들이 김 실장의 발언 이후 화요일에 한국 주식을 대량 매도했다고 보도됐다. 그의 발언이 매도의 유일한 원인은 아닐지 몰라도 시장은 이러한 신호에 매우 민감하다. 정부의 책무 중 하나는 시장경제의 원칙을 수호하여 국민이 자신 있게 투자할 수 있도록 하는 것이다.

editorials.title: 'Citizen dividend'
editorials.body: Top presidential aide's proposal raises fears of anti-market thinking Kim Yong-beon, the presidential chief of staff for policy, on Tuesday proposed a "citizen dividend" scheme that would return part of the windfall tax revenue generated by the semiconductor boom to the public. After the proposal triggered controversy, the presidential office brushed it off as Kim’s personal opinion that had not been discussed internally. Nevertheless, the abrupt and radical suggestion from the government’s top policymaker bewildered people who place their trust in a capitalist market economy. Kim wrote on Facebook that the fruits of the artificial intelligence infrastructure era are not generated by a handful of companies alone, but rest on an industrial foundation built collectively by the Korean people over the past half-century, adding that part of those gains should be systematically returned to the public. He mentioned "citizen dividends" as a concrete way of putting the idea into practice. First, the anti-market undertones contained in the concept of distributing profits in the form of dividends to every citizen are deeply concerning. The argument that semiconductor companies have grown on the back of special legislation, public infrastructure such as roads, electricity and water supply, and tax benefits is understandable to some extent. But that is entirely different from saying that those profits should therefore be distributed to the public. In 2023, Samsung Electronics suffered a 15 trillion won ($10 billion) loss in its semiconductor business alone. The government and the public did not absorb any of those losses then. A structure in which corporate "windfall profits" are shared with the public, while companies alone bear the losses, is inconsistent with both fairness and basic economic principles. Instead, Kim’s proposal gives the impression that the government also intends to take a slice of the hundreds of trillions of won in operating profits expected this year at Samsung Electronics and SK hynix, as labor unions, subcontractors, farmers and fisher groups, and even politicians, scramble for a share. When companies earn profits, the government collects its due in corporate taxes. This is the mechanism through which profits are redistributed to society in a capitalist market economy. If the government seeks to take corporate profits by means other than taxes, it would need a legal basis enacted through legislation. Otherwise, it would amount to unfair double taxation, nearing coercion. If there is excess tax revenue, it should primarily be used to pay down the national debt in line with the principles of fiscal management. Redistributing excess tax revenue to the people under the label of a "dividend" invites suspicions of a populist attempt at winning favor with cash payments. Companies are fulfilling their social obligations by paying taxes and creating jobs. If a company still has profits left after covering its costs, including taxes, debt repayments, payments to subcontractors and labor expenses, those profits belong to the shareholders. If profits are to be "shared with society," the decision likewise belongs to the shareholders. Viewed from the perspective of enhancing industrial competitiveness, Kim’s understanding of the issue is overly complacent. Not only is the AI-driven semiconductor boom unlikely to continue forever, but major competitors such as the United States and China are aggressively closing the gap. In these circumstances, is it not the role of the president’s chief economic aide to worry first about strategies for maintaining or expanding the lead? For tech companies, profits should be used first and foremost for investment in new technologies and facilities. Companies that fail to do so are quickly driven out of the market. Now is not the time to kill the goose that lays the golden eggs, but to raise more such geese. Foreign investors reportedly dumped Korean stocks in large quantities on Tuesday after Kim’s remarks. His words may not have been the sole reason for the sell-off, but markets are highly sensitive to such signals. One of the government’s responsibilities is to safeguard the principles of the market economy so people can invest with confidence. editorials.pullQuote: A structure where corporate 'windfall profits' are shared with the public, while companies alone bear losses, is inconsistent with fairness.
summary: Presidential aide Kim Yong-beon proposed a 'citizen dividend' scheme to share semiconductor windfall profits with the public.
Editorial warns the plan threatens market principles and unfairly shifts corporate profits away from shareholders.
Critics argue profits should fund R&D and debt, not populist cash payments, as global competition intensifies.
editorials.stance: The editorial opposes the citizen dividend proposal as anti-market, economically unsound, and strategically misguided during global tech competition.

editorial.title: [사설] 조정식 국회의장 후보, 명실상부한 입법부 수장 되길
editorial.body: 더불어민주당이 22대 국회 후반기 국회의장 후보로 6선의 조정식 의원을 13일 선출했다. 조 의원은 1차 투표에서 각각 5선인 박지원·김태년 의원을 꺾고 당선을 확정 지은 뒤 “빛의 혁명이 어둠을 물리치고 대한민국을 정상으로 이끌었듯이 후반기 국회를 대한민국 대전환에
editorial.pullQuote: 빛의 혁명이 어둠을 물리치고 대한민국을 정상으로 이끌었듯이 후반기 국회를 대한민국 대전환에 이끌겠다.
summary: 더불어민주당이 조정식 의원을 22대 국회 후반기 의장 후보로 선출했다.
조 의원은 1차 투표에서 박지원·김태년 의원을 꺾고 당선을 확정했다.
국회 후반기를 대한민국 대전환으로 이끌겠다는 입장을 밝혔다.
editorial.stance: 조정식 의장 후보가 명실상부한 입법부 수장으로서 역할을 기대한다.
editorial.titleTr: [Editorial] Jo Jeong-sik's Bid for Speaker: Time to Prove Leadership of the Legislature
summaryTr: The Democratic Party of Korea has elected Rep. Jo Jeong-sik as its candidate for National Assembly speaker for the second half of the 22nd parliament.
Rep. Jo secured his election in the first round of voting, defeating Reps. Park Ji-won and Kim Tae-nyeon.
He expressed his stance to lead the second half of the National Assembly toward a great transformation of the Republic of Korea.
editorial.bodyTr: The Democratic Party of Korea elected six-term Rep. Cho Jung-sik as its candidate for National Assembly speaker for the second half of the 22nd National Assembly on the 13th. Rep. Cho secured his victory in the first round of voting by defeating five-term Reps. Park Ji-won and Kim Tae-yeon, then stated that "just as the revolution of light vanquished darkness and led South Korea back on the right track, I will steer the latter half of this National Assembly toward a great transformation of the nation.

editorial.titleTr: Semiconductor strike threatens national economy...government right to prepare emergency intervention powers
summaryTr: Samsung Electronics labor negotiations collapse, total strike planned for 18 days starting the 21st
Semiconductor industry damage estimated at 30-40 trillion won, with 1,770 affiliated companies facing cascade effects
Government invokes emergency adjustment authority as measures needed to respond to national economic crisis
editorial.bodyTr:
The worst-case scenario that could paralyze the heart of a semiconductor powerhouse is becoming reality. Samsung Electronics' labor and management concluded a grueling 17-hour marathon negotiation over performance bonuses in a breakdown early on the 13th. An 18-day general strike is now set to begin on the 21st. The damage is estimated at 30 trillion won by the union's calculation and 40 trillion won by securities firms. Over 1,770 subcontractors will face cascading blows, and the regional economies of Pyeongtaek and Hwaseong, where factories are located, are bracing for impact.

This is not merely a labor dispute between a company and its workers. It is a crisis that directly threatens the competitiveness of the nation's semiconductor industry and economic security. Samsung Electronics accounts for the majority of South Korea's chip exports. Any disruption in its production immediately ripples through global supply chains. Competitors in Taiwan and the United States are watching closely, hoping to capitalize on any opening.

The tragedy is that both sides saw this coming but failed to prevent it. Management prioritized shareholder returns over worker compensation. The union made demands without fully considering the company's circumstances. In the end, it is the nation and its people who will pay the price for this breakdown in communication and compromise.

editorials.bodyTr: The youth employment market continues its seemingly endless downward slide. The April employment rate for young people aged 15-29 stood at 43.7 percent, down 1.6 percentage points from the same month last year, marking the 24th consecutive month of decline. This represents the longest losing streak in 17 years since November 2009, immediately following the global financial crisis. It is a dangerous signal that employment opportunities are disappearing for young people who will shoulder South Korea's future. editorials.titleTr: Academic Achievement Ranks 3rd, Mental Health 34th—Children's Happiness Is Key to Developed Nation Status
summaryTr: South Korean children rank 3rd in academic ability but 34th in mental health, revealing a stark gap
Asymmetric reality confirmed: children excel academically but lack happiness
Children's happiness is the true measure of an advanced nation
editorials.bodyTr: South Korean children and adolescents rank at the top in academic ability but at the bottom in mental health and life satisfaction—a reality confirmed once again by new figures. According to UNICEF's "Report Card 20," which compares the health status and academic performance of children and adolescents across major nations, South Korea ranked third in academic ability but only 34th in mental health. The extreme imbalance—doing well in studies but unhappy—reveals what our society is demanding of our children and what we are failing to provide them.
